data.body:
PASS-1 review — rubric T8 (LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe, overlay): CLEAN, no findings.

The overlay applies: this plan defines an LLM sub-system — a single-turn structured-output call, an output schema `[{move_id, subject, finding_refs[]}]`, a MOVE REGISTRY enum, and a deterministic SUBJECT VALIDATOR decision-rule. I tool-grounded every referenced schema/enum/agent/protocol against the codebase; each resolves to a concrete, fully-specified entry, so emitting no T8 finding is the correct outcome.

Tool-grounded confirmation (no proven structural gap on any of the five T8 probes):

(a) No schema/enum referenced whose value vocabulary is undefined.
- `move_id` enum vocabulary is fully enumerated (moves 1-9, 11, 12 with locked templates) in the plan's §What and realized verbatim in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:56-101` (`MOVE_REGISTRY`); project extensions via `.rebar/plan_review_moves.json` are validated (must carry a `{subject}` placeholder, `load_move_registry` orchestrator.py:104-122).
- The `subject` free-text field — the one field the template interpolates — has its value vocabulary fully bounded by the SUBJECT VALIDATOR (≤8 words / ≤60 chars; reject code tokens `( ) { } ; = \` import`; reject leading imperative verb), specified inline in §What and matching `_validate_subject` + `_IMPERATIVE_STARTS` (passes.py:539-570).
- `finding_refs` resolves to the orchestrator-minted content-fingerprint `id` — `ca03` (dry-frost-zenith, CLOSED) is the SOLE owner (`mint_finding_id`); this coach is a stated CONSUMER and `db7b`'s sidecar RECORDS the same id. Non-circular; verified against both tickets.

(b) The processing protocol/decision-rule is co-located with the schema that needs it: the SUBJECT VALIDATOR and the REJECT→no-coaching rule sit alongside the output schema in §What; the deterministic-render rule (template + subject) is co-located with the registry definition.

(c) No counter/state increment with ambiguous placement: there are no counters; cap ownership is explicitly disambiguated — Pass-3 owns the top-N cap and the block decision, and "the coach never alters the cap or the block decision" (§Scope).

(d) A fallback for an incomplete/failed sub-step IS specified: on a rejected subject, "emit NO coaching for that finding — best-effort drop, never a retry-into-prose loop" (§What), confirmed at passes.py:525-526; and a finding matching no move yields no coaching (AC6), confirmed at passes.py:523-526.

(e) No instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipattern; referenced agents exist and are fully specified: `ca03` (orchestrator/id-minter) and `9da1` (the shared three-pass framework this is the coaching layer for) both exist and reciprocally specify the mint-once/consume id contract. The "name-the-move-not-the-answer" property is baked into the human-authored deterministic template (orchestrator.py:56-101), not delegated to LLM judgment — so the LLM authors no prose and there is no pink-elephant negative instruction load-bearing the property.

AFFIRMATIONS (T8 criteria this chunk passes): no undefined-but-referenced enum/protocol; decision-rule co-located with the schema; no ambiguous counter placement; an explicit best-effort fallback for the rejected-subject / no-move sub-steps; referenced agents/enums exist and are fully specified (tool-verified). 

NOTE (not a T8 finding, flagged for the author / a consistency criterion): §What and AC1 say the output is "CLUSTERED by move", but the output schema is one note per finding and no clustering step appears in `pass4_coach` (passes.py:493-536) or the schema. This is an output-presentation/ordering property — not an undefined enum, missing protocol, ambiguous counter, or unspecified fallback — so it does not rise to a proven T8 structural gap; recording it only so the clustering claim and the per-finding emit shape can be reconciled by the relevant pass.
